what_happened: GitHub Actions workflow used tag-based references (e.g., @v6) instead of pinned SHA commits
why: Tag references are mutable and can be changed by action maintainers, creating supply-chain security risks
fix_applied: Replaced all tag references with full commit SHAs and added version comment annotations (e.g., # v4.2.2)
pattern_to_remember: When pinning GitHub Actions to SHAs: the file may show @v6 but the canonical SHAs provided correspond to v4 releases. Always verify which SHA corresponds to which version using the comment annotation. Format: uses: actions/checkout@<full-sha> # v<major.minor.patch>
